steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the subject of the clause beginning after the colon. The colon's previous subject is "dark fiction," a singular noun. The blank must be a pronoun that agrees with "dark fiction" and fits Standard English grammar.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'one uses': "One" refers to a generic person, not to "dark fiction." It doesn't logically continue the sentence about what dark fiction does. ✗
  • Choice B — 'it uses': "It" is a singular pronoun that correctly refers back to "dark fiction," and the resulting clause — dark fiction uses horror tropes to challenge familiar ways of knowing — is grammatically and logically sound. ✓
  • Choice C — 'they use': "They" is plural, but "dark fiction" is singular. Pronoun-antecedent disagreement. ✗
  • Choice D — 'we use': "We" shifts the subject to the readers or a group of people, changing the meaning so that readers — not dark fiction itself — are the ones using horror tropes. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
